description: Profiling-derived rules for kernel performance analysis. Based on common rocprof-compute findings and AMD GPU performance counters.

rules.name: Low MFMA Utilization
rules.description: MFMA unit utilization is below expected threshold. For GEMM kernels, MFMA should be >70% busy to be considered compute-bound.
rules.diagnosis: The kernel may be memory-bound or have excessive synchronization between MFMA instructions.
rules.suggestion: Increase tile size to improve data reuse. Use software pipelining to overlap loads with MFMA. Ensure MFMA instructions are properly chained without unnecessary waitcnts.

rules.name: Low VALU Utilization
rules.description: VALU (Vector ALU) utilization is below threshold. Wavefronts may be stalled on memory or synchronization.
rules.diagnosis: Check for excessive s_waitcnt, barrier overhead, or memory-bound execution.
rules.suggestion: Overlap computation with memory operations. Reduce barriers. Consider loop unrolling to increase ILP.

rules.name: Memory Bandwidth Saturation
rules.description: HBM bandwidth usage is at or near peak. The kernel is memory-bandwidth-bound.
rules.diagnosis: The kernel's arithmetic intensity is too low relative to its data movement. Memory traffic may include redundant or uncoalesced accesses.
rules.suggestion: Increase data reuse via larger tiles and LDS buffering. Ensure coalesced access patterns. Consider using lower precision (FP8 instead of FP16) to halve data movement. Use compression if supported.

rules.name: LDS Bank Conflict Rate
rules.description: LDS access patterns cause bank conflicts. Conflict rate above threshold indicates significant serialization.
rules.diagnosis: Shared memory access patterns have stride conflicts. Common with power-of-2 strides matching the 32-bank LDS.
rules.suggestion: Add padding to shared memory arrays (+1 to one dimension). Rearrange access patterns to distribute across banks.

rules.name: Low Occupancy
rules.description: Kernel occupancy (active waves / max waves) is too low. This reduces the GPU's ability to hide latency.
rules.diagnosis: Excessive VGPR or LDS usage per workgroup limits concurrent wavefronts.
rules.suggestion: Reduce register usage with __launch_bounds__. Decrease LDS allocation per workgroup. Consider smaller tile sizes. Check for register spilling.

rules.name: Excessive Wavefront Stalls
rules.description: Wavefronts spend a significant portion of time stalled, waiting for memory or synchronization.
rules.diagnosis: Common causes: s_waitcnt vmcnt(0) too often, s_barrier overhead, memory system congestion.
rules.suggestion: Use partial waitcnts. Prefetch data. Reduce barrier frequency. Consider persistent kernel patterns to avoid launch overhead.

rules.name: L2 Cache Thrashing
rules.description: L2 cache hit rate is very low, indicating that the working set exceeds L2 capacity or access patterns are adversarial.
rules.diagnosis: Data reuse is poor. Adjacent workgroups may be accessing distant memory regions.
rules.suggestion: Improve data locality through tiling. Use software-managed caching in LDS. Consider workgroup scheduling that improves spatial locality.

rules.name: Instruction Mix Imbalance
rules.description: The ratio of compute instructions to memory instructions is outside the optimal range for the hardware's compute-to-bandwidth ratio.
rules.diagnosis: Too low: kernel is memory-bound, not enough computation per byte loaded. Too high: compute is excessive, consider reducing precision or algorithm change.
rules.suggestion: For memory-bound (ratio < 2): increase tile size, use data compression, reduce precision. For compute-bound (ratio > 100): check if algorithm can be simplified.

rules.name: Unbalanced Workgroup Distribution
rules.description: Some CUs are doing significantly more work than others, leaving some CUs idle while others are overloaded.
rules.diagnosis: Grid dimensions don't evenly divide across CUs, or work per workgroup varies significantly.
rules.suggestion: Pad grid dimensions to be multiples of CU count. Use Stream-K scheduling for better load balancing. Ensure uniform work per workgroup.

rules.name: Register Spilling to Scratch
rules.description: Kernel spills registers to scratch memory (off-chip). Scratch access has similar latency to global memory, severely impacting performance.
rules.diagnosis: Kernel exceeds available VGPR/SGPR allocation, forcing register spills through the memory hierarchy.
rules.suggestion: Reduce register usage. Simplify control flow. Use __launch_bounds__ to guide register allocation. Break large kernels into smaller ones.
